title: String Operations
description: Practice essential string manipulation techniques in Java by checking if a string is a palindrome. String handling is crucial for validating text in web elements and processing test data in automation frameworks.
instructions: Create a method `isPalindrome()` that accepts a String parameter
Convert the string to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
Remove all spaces and special characters (keep only letters and digits)
Check if the string reads the same forwards and backwards
Return true if it's a palindrome, false otherwise
learningObjectives: Master String class methods in Java
Learn StringBuilder usage for string manipulation

testCases.name: Palindrome with spaces
input: A man a plan a canal Panama

hints.content: Use String methods: toLowerCase() to handle case-insensitivity and replaceAll("[^a-z0-9]", "") to remove non-alphanumeric characters using regex.
Use two pointers approach: start from both ends (left=0, right=length-1) and compare characters while moving towards the center. If any mismatch is found, return false.

Complete solution: Clean the string, use a while loop with two pointers (left < right), compare charAt(left) with charAt(right), increment left and decrement right. Return true if loop completes without finding mismatches.
